tasks.title: Create feature branch and verify clean working tree
tasks.description: Hard gate: create a dedicated git branch for the game_mode feature before any other work begins. No subsequent task may proceed without this branch being checked out.
tasks.details:
Run these commands one at a time and verify each before continuing:

git status

git checkout main

git pull --ff-only

git checkout -b feature/game-mode-setting

git branch --show-current

The final command must output exactly: feature/game-mode-setting

If `git status` shows uncommitted changes at the start, STOP and surface them to the user. Do not stash or discard. Reference the Superpowers skill `using-git-worktrees` if parallel work is needed.

Note: base branch was changed from `update-002` to `main` — `update-002` no longer exists in this repo; `main` is the active integration branch.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `git branch --show-current` and confirm output is `feature/game-mode-setting`. Run `git status` and confirm working tree is clean.

tasks.title: Add settings table, DAO, and migration
tasks.description: Introduce a single-row `app_settings` table with a `game_mode` column (default 'easy'). This is the persistence layer only — no UI yet.
tasks.details:
Files touched (max 3):
1. `database/schema.py` — add `app_settings` table DDL with columns: `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1)`, `game_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'easy' CHECK (game_mode IN ('easy', 'realism'))`, `updated_at TEXT NOT NULL`. Add an idempotent `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO app_settings (id, game_mode, updated_at) VALUES (1, 'easy', datetime('now'))` to seed the row.
2. `database/settings_dao.py` (new) — expose `get_game_mode() -> str` and `set_game_mode(mode: str) -> None`. Both must validate mode is in {'easy', 'realism'} and use the shared SQLite connection.
3. `tests/test_settings_dao.py` (new) — unit tests for get/set, default value, invalid mode rejection.

Use the existing connection pattern (WAL mode, shared connection). Do NOT add any new env vars. Keep changes idempotent — running schema init twice must not error.

Reference Superpowers skill: `test-driven-development` — write the DAO tests first, then implement.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pytest tests/test_settings_dao.py -v` — all tests pass. Manually: delete `am4_data.db`, run `python main.py dashboard --db am4_data.db`, then `sqlite3 am4_data.db "SELECT * FROM app_settings;"` — confirm one row with game_mode='easy'. Run init a second time, confirm no error and row unchanged.

tasks.title: Add /settings page with game_mode dropdown
tasks.description: Create a Settings page route and template exposing the game_mode selector as a dropdown (Easy / Realism). Save via HTMX, no full page reload.
tasks.details:
Files touched (max 3):
1. `routes/settings.py` (new) — FastAPI router with `GET /settings` (renders page) and `POST /settings/game-mode` (HTMX endpoint, accepts form field `game_mode`, calls `settings_dao.set_game_mode`, returns the updated dropdown fragment). Mount router in `main.py` (single-line addition).
2. `templates/settings.html` (new) — extends base layout. Section titled "Game Mode" with a `<select>` bound to `hx-post="/settings/game-mode"`, `hx-trigger="change"`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"`. Include a short helper text: "Easy mode disables runway-length validation and uses the easy-mode contribution multiplier (1.0). Realism mode enforces runway constraints and uses contribution multiplier 1.5."
3. `templates/_partials/game_mode_select.html` (new) — extracted dropdown partial so the HTMX swap returns just the select element with the new selected value.

Add a nav entry for Settings if one does not exist (treat as a fourth file ONLY if base nav template needs editing — otherwise skip).

Do not gate any behavior yet — that is Task 4. This task is purely the persistence-backed UI.
tasks.testStrategy: Smoke test: start dashboard with `python main.py dashboard --db am4_data.db`, navigate to `/settings`, confirm dropdown shows current mode. Change to 'realism', confirm no full page reload, confirm SQL `SELECT game_mode FROM app_settings` returns 'realism'. Reload page, confirm dropdown still reads 'realism'. Change back to 'easy'.

tasks.title: Gate runway validation on /my-routes by game_mode
tasks.description: Skip the 'Aircraft runway requirement exceeds destination runway length' validation when game_mode is 'easy'. Show it only in 'realism'.
tasks.details:
Files touched (max 2):
1. `routes/my_routes.py` (or wherever the runway check currently lives — locate by grepping for the exact warning string "Aircraft runway requirement exceeds destination runway length"). Wrap the check in `if settings_dao.get_game_mode() == 'realism':`. Cache the mode lookup per-request if the check runs in a hot loop.
2. `templates/my_routes.html` — if the warning is rendered template-side, ensure the template branch is also mode-aware (pass `game_mode` into the template context once via `base_context`).

Do NOT remove the validation logic — only gate it. Realism users must still see the warning.

Reference Superpowers skill: `verification-before-completion` — after the change, manually exercise BOTH modes before marking done.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Set mode='easy' via /settings. Open /my-routes, enter Hub=LHR, Dest=OUA, Aircraft=b748. Click Save. Confirm no runway warning appears and the row saves successfully. 2. Set mode='realism' via /settings. Repeat the same input. Confirm the red 'Aircraft runway requirement exceeds destination runway length.' warning appears and the save is blocked. 3. Confirm existing valid route entries (e.g. LHR→JFK with b748) save in both modes.

tasks.title: Audit am4 package calls and thread game_mode through profit calculations
tasks.description: Find every call site that invokes the `am4` package for route/profit calculation and ensure the `realism` (and `fourx` where applicable) flag matches the user's game_mode setting. The contribution multiplier differs by 1.5x between modes — wrong mode = wrong profit numbers everywhere.
tasks.details:
This is an investigation + targeted edit task. Steps:

1. Grep the codebase for `am4.utils`, `AircraftRoute`, `FAPIReqUser`, `realism=`, and any function that returns profit/contribution. Produce a short inventory in a scratch markdown file under `scripts/scratch/am4-mode-audit.md` listing each call site with file:line.

2. For each call site, determine the correct mode-aware parameter (typically `realism: bool`). The am4 docs confirm `realism` is on `FAPIReqUser` and the contribution formula uses `k_gm = 1.5 if realism else 1`.

3. Replace hardcoded mode assumptions with `settings_dao.get_game_mode() == 'realism'`. Add a thin helper `core/game_mode.py` (new file) exposing `is_realism() -> bool` and `as_am4_kwargs() -> dict` returning `{'realism': bool, 'fourx': bool}` to keep call sites tidy.

4. Re-run extraction or recompute on a known route (e.g. LHR→JFK b748) in both modes and confirm the contribution component differs by 1.5x as expected.

Files touched: `core/game_mode.py` (new) plus 1-2 call-site files. If the audit reveals more than 3 call sites need editing, STOP and split into a follow-up task — do not exceed the 3-file budget in one commit.

Reference: am4 formulae doc — contribution multiplier k_gm = 1.5 (realism) vs 1 (easy).
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Pick a known route like LHR→JFK with b748. Note current profit/AC/day on /my-routes in easy mode. 2. Switch to realism via /settings. Hard-refresh /my-routes. 3. Profit number must change (typically increase due to 1.5x contribution multiplier in realism). 4. Switch back to easy, confirm number returns to original. 5. Spot-check the new number against in-game profit on a single live route to confirm the math now matches what the user actually sees in AM4.

tasks.title: Add game_mode badge to base layout nav
tasks.description: Show the active mode in the top nav so the user always knows which constraints/multipliers are in effect. Avoids silent confusion when profit numbers shift after a mode change.
tasks.details:
Files touched (max 2):
1. `templates/base.html` (or wherever the top nav lives) — add a small badge near the page title: `EASY` (green) or `REALISM` (amber). Tailwind classes: `bg-emerald-900 text-emerald-200` for easy, `bg-amber-900 text-amber-200` for realism. Make the badge a link to `/settings`.
2. `core/context.py` (or wherever `base_context` is defined) — add `game_mode` to the default context dict so every template has access without per-route changes.

Note: the `base_context(request, None)` stub mentioned in known issues is the natural place to do this. Do NOT fix the hub freshness banners in this task — keep scope tight.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Navigate to /my-routes, /my-fleet, /my-hubs, / — badge appears on every page with current mode. 2. Click badge — lands on /settings. 3. Change mode in /settings, navigate to any other page — badge reflects new mode. 4. Confirm badge does not break layout on narrow viewports.

tasks.title: Final verification, commit hygiene check, and merge prep
tasks.description: Walk the full feature end-to-end in both modes, confirm all commits are clean and atomic, and prepare the branch for merge into update-002.
tasks.details:
Reference Superpowers skill: `finishing-a-development-branch`.

Steps:

1. End-to-end smoke in EASY mode: start dashboard, /settings shows easy, /my-routes accepts LHR→OUA b748 save, profit numbers visible, badge shows EASY.

2. End-to-end smoke in REALISM mode: switch via /settings, /my-routes blocks LHR→OUA b748 with runway warning, profit numbers on existing routes shift (contribution multiplier kicked in), badge shows REALISM.

3. Commit hygiene: run `git log --oneline main..HEAD` and confirm one commit per task (Tasks 2-6 = 5 commits minimum). Squash only if commits are clearly broken intermediate states.

4. Run full test suite: `pytest tests/ -v` — all green.

5. Run `git diff main --stat` and visually scan for unintended changes (stray prints, commented code, scratch files outside `scripts/scratch/`).

6. Push branch: `git push -u origin feature/game-mode-setting`

7. Surface to user: report pass/fail of every step above, list any deferred items (e.g. "Top Extracted panel still does not filter by runway in realism mode — separate task recommended"), and recommend merge or further work.

Do NOT auto-merge. Surface and wait for user direction.
tasks.testStrategy: All 7 steps above must pass. The verification report given to the user must explicitly state the easy-mode profit number and realism-mode profit number for one identical route, demonstrating the multiplier is now active.
